bench: awq_gemv_bf16_hip microbench (scripts/bench/bench_awq_gemv.py), conc=1 M=1 decode GEMV, synthetic int4 weights, R9700 single-card
correctness: cos=1.00000 vs dequant+matmul reference on all shapes
findings: Kernel runs 18-50% of memory roofline; efficiency rises with N (output dim) because num_blocks = N/256 — small-N projections (N=5120 -> 20 blocks) underfill the 64-CU GPU.
SPLIT_K (warps-within-block K-split) is the existing knob: sk=1 is catastrophic (2%, latency-bound), climbs to ~18-34% by sk=16, then PLATEAUS under roofline -> a second cap beyond occupancy.
Likely caps: (a) grid too small for small N (only ~20 blocks); (b) per-int4 dequant ALU (~6 float ops/weight) + scalar 4-byte qweight loads (no 128-bit vectorization).
next_levers: Grid-level split-K: launch (N/256)*SK blocks each computing a K-slice partial + a reduction, instead of SK warps within N/256 blocks -> fills the 64 CUs for small N.
Vectorize qweight loads to uint4 (128-bit) and cut dequant ALU (fold zero-point / use bf16 dot intrinsics).
